message:
wa-nu4.3.4.2: watcher health snapshot in CLI with stuck pane detection

Add `wa robot health` command for agent-accessible health snapshots.
Add per-pane activity tracking and stuck pane detection (>5min threshold)
to HealthSnapshot diagnostics. Add 12 new tests covering stuck pane
detection, schema stability, backward compatibility, and CLI smoke.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.9","title":"Remote setup + wezterm config generator tests (fixtures, dry-run behavior, logging)","description":"# Task: Remote setup + config generator tests

## Goal
Ensure remote setup and config generation remain safe and deterministic.

## Testing
- Config generator fixture tests:
  - inserts WA block
  - updates WA block
  - does not duplicate

- Remote setup command-building tests:
  - produces the expected ssh/scp invocations in dry-run mode
  - redacts sensitive values in logs
  - emits step boundaries + durations so failures are diagnosable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests do not require real SSH connectivity.
- Logging output includes step boundaries + durations.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4","title":"[EPIC] Diagnostic Bundle + Crash Capture","description":"# [EPIC] Diagnostic Bundle + Crash Capture

## Mission
Make it easy to answer "Is wa healthy?" and "Why did it do that?" without guesswork, including crash-only diagnostics.

## Deliverables
- `wa doctor` checks (environment + config + DB)
- health snapshot plumbing from the watcher
- diagnostic bundle export (sanitized)
- crash bundle capture + listing/triage integration
- performance budget checks in CI

## Deferred
- Incident/reproduce workflows are deferred until demand (see wa-upg.1).

## Testing & observability (non-negotiable)
Diagnostics tooling is only valuable if it is reliable and safe.

- Unit/integration tests must verify:
  - doctor checks against fixtures (no real WezTerm required)
  - diagnostic/crash bundle contents and layout
  - redaction: bundles/logs never leak secrets

- E2E scripts must verify:
  - `wa doctor` behaves correctly in healthy vs broken scenarios
  - crashes produce actionable messages and point to artifacts

## Success Criteria
- `wa doctor`/`wa status` provide actionable health signals (DB connectivity, queue depths, ingest lag, lock state).
- Diagnostic/crash bundles include logs, effective config, and redacted diagnostics for bug reports.
- Performance budgets exist for core hot paths and regressions are visible in CI.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","title":"[Human command] `wa doctor` (environment + config + DB checks)","description":"# Task: Human command wa doctor

## Goal
Provide a deterministic checklist that validates the wa environment.

Doctor is a user trust surface: it must make failures actionable and help users understand where wa stores state.

## Checks (initial)
- Workspace/config resolution
  - print resolved workspace root
  - print resolved DB/log/lock/socket paths
  - verify workspace is writable

- WezTerm
  - wezterm CLI available (wezterm cli list works)
  - wezterm version meets minimum supported
  - scrollback settings are sufficient (warn if too low; best-effort)

- Storage
  - DB is readable/writable
  - schema/migration version matches
  - WAL + foreign_keys configured

- Watcher status (if running)
  - last tick time
  - tailer count
  - backlog sizes

- Feature availability
  - browser (playwright)
  - mcp
  - web/tui/sync (optional)

## Output
- Rich panels when TTY.
- Non-TTY JSON suitable for automation.

## Safety
- Output must never include secrets (even if config contains tokens).
- Redaction must be applied to any potentially sensitive field.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests: `wa-nu4.3.4.8` (fixture-based checks + output stability)
- E2E: `wa-4vx.10.22` (healthy/broken scenarios with verbose artifacts)

## Acceptance Criteria
- On a healthy system, wa doctor exits 0 with a concise summary.
- On a broken system, it exits non-zero and lists actionable fixes.
- Output never includes secrets (even if config contains tokens).

Added --json flag that outputs structured JSON with all diagnostic checks, runtime health, crash history, and circuit breakers. JSON includes: ok (bool), status (ok/warning/error), version, checks array with name/status/detail/recommendation per check, optional circuits array. Plain text output preserved unchanged. Also fixed pre-existing test issue (verbose: false → verbose: 0 type mismatch). All tests pass: 20 wa tests, 1139+101 wa-core tests.","created_at":"2026-01-29T06:42:38Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.2","title":"Expose watcher health snapshot in CLI (status/health command)","description":"# Task: Expose watcher health snapshot

## Goal
Make the watcher’s health state visible so we can quickly diagnose:
- stuck tailers
- slow polling
- DB backpressure
- pattern match overload

## Metrics (example)
- discovery tick duration
- tailer count + per-domain breakdown
- per-pane last_seen + last_seq
- storage write queue depth
- event bus queue depths
- FTS insert latency

## Deliverables
- `wa status --health` (or `wa health`) prints the snapshot.
- Robot/MCP can access the same snapshot if useful.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - health snapshot JSON is schema-parseable and stable
  - “stuck pane” synthetic scenario is surfaced clearly

- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (stable JSON output in non-TTY mode)

- Diagnostic coverage:
  - `wa doctor` tests (`wa-nu4.3.4.8`) should validate that health signals are consistent with watcher state when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- When a pane stops producing output, health snapshot highlights it clearly.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.3","title":"Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized)

## Goal
Generate a single artifact users can attach to bug reports that contains:
- environment info (OS, wa version)
- config summary (redacted)
- DB health stats (counts, schema version, WAL size)
- last N events + workflow step logs (redacted)
- **active pane reservations + recent reservation conflicts (redacted)**

## Safety requirements
- Never include secrets.
- Apply PolicyEngine redaction rules to any text fields.
- Prefer summaries over raw transcripts.

## Output
- A directory or archive (e.g. `.tar.zst`) written to a user-specified path.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests: `wa-nu4.3.4.7` validates:
  - redaction (no secrets)
  - stable file layout
  - stable metadata fields
  - works while watcher is running

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle can be generated while watcher is running.
- Bundle contents are stable and documented.


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.9.2: Implement config loader: defaults + file + env + CLI overrides; show effective config ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.6: Retention & hygiene: prune old segments, maintenance log, explicit vacuum command ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.2: DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.5: Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.7: Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.4","title":"Perf budgets + regression checks (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS inserts)","description":"# Task: Perf budgets + regression checks

## Goal
Define and enforce basic performance budgets so wa stays fast as features accrete.

## Key hot paths
- Quick reject / prefiltering (if applicable)
- Delta extraction + seq assignment
- Pattern detection over deltas
- DB writes (segments + FTS)

## Deliverables
- Criterion benches for hot paths (some may already exist; extend to cover end-to-end ingest tick).
- Budget thresholds (warn or fail) suitable for CI.

## Testing / CI expectations
- Use coarse budgets to avoid noisy failures.
- CI should detect gross regressions (order-of-magnitude) reliably and upload benchmark artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI can detect a gross regression (e.g., 10× slowdown) reliably.
- Benches are stable (avoid noisy wall-clock assertions; use coarse budgets).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","title":"Metrics endpoint (Prometheus): wa watch --metrics bind + core counters/histograms","description":"# Task: Metrics endpoint (Prometheus)

## Goal
Expose optional Prometheus metrics to support long-running reliability:
- ingest lag
- gaps
- queue depths
- pattern detection latency
- workflow outcomes
- DB write latencies

## Design
- Disabled by default.
- Bind address configurable (default localhost).
- Must not significantly slow down hot path.

## Safety
- Default bind is localhost-only.
- Binding publicly requires an explicit, scary flag (proposed): `--dangerous-bind-any`.
- Metrics must never include transcript contents or secrets.

## Deliverables
- `wa watch --metrics <bind>` and/or config-driven enable.
- A minimal set of stable metric names.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - metric names are stable
  - registry can be instantiated with metrics disabled (zero overhead path)

- Integration tests:
  - start watcher with metrics enabled (bind to localhost ephemeral port)
  - fetch `/metrics` and assert:
    - response is valid Prometheus text format
    - key metrics appear (even if counts are zero)

- Safety tests:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - enabling metrics does not leak secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- When enabled, `/metrics` responds with valid Prometheus text format.
- When disabled, zero overhead on hot path.
","notes":"Review: repo already includes metrics endpoint implementation and CLI flags (files: crates/wa-core/src/metrics.rs, crates/wa/src/main.rs, metrics config).  {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.6","title":"CI e2e job: run scripts with verbose logging, keep artifacts on failure","description":"# Task: CI e2e job

## Goal
Run end-to-end tests automatically when possible and make failures diagnosable.

## Design
- Add a CI job that:
  - installs/ensures WezTerm (if feasible) OR runs in an environment where WezTerm exists
  - runs `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all --verbose`
  - captures artifacts directory
  - uploads artifacts on failure

## Failure UX
- CI output should point directly to the artifacts.
- The e2e script should print a concise summary at the end.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job must execute registry cases (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all`) and retain artifacts.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A failing e2e run produces an artifact bundle that includes wa logs + wezterm logs + exports.


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Add a mode (or a dedicated small case) that intentionally fails so we can verify artifact upload behavior.
  - Ensure the job prints the artifacts path/URL in the log summary.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.7","title":"CI e2e (extended): run approval allow-once scenario + keep artifacts","description":"# Task: CI extended e2e job

## Goal
Run the phase-4 "approval allow-once" E2E scenario in CI once the feature set is available.

This job specifically validates:
- RequireApproval UX payload
- wa approve command
- approval storage
- audited outcomes + redaction

## Behavior
- Execute:
  - ./scripts/e2e_test.sh --case approval-allow-once --verbose
- Always upload artifacts when failing.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job extends the registry run with additional approval/allow-once scenarios.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A CI failure includes enough artifacts to diagnose whether the bug is in:
  - policy decision
  - approval granting
  - retry behavior
  - audit/export


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job logs include the artifacts upload location.
  - Consider a “known fail” dry run to verify artifact upload and summary formatting.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.8","title":"CI setup job: run wa setup idempotency E2E (temp home, artifacts on failure)","description":"# Task: CI setup job (dotfile patching safety)

## Goal
Continuously validate that wa setup is safe and idempotent.

Dotfile patching is one of the fastest ways to lose user trust. This CI job ensures:
- dry-run does not write
- apply mode changes only the intended block
- re-running is a no-op

## Behavior
- Run the setup idempotency E2E:
  - ./scripts/e2e_setup_test.sh --all --verbose
  - or reuse ./scripts/e2e_test.sh if we unify harnesses

- Always upload artifacts on failure:
  - before/after copies
  - diffs
  - logs

## Dependencies
- Uses the E2E setup test described in wa-nu4.3.3.10.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job focuses on setup idempotency cases from the registry.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI failures include all artifacts needed to debug the patcher step.


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job asserts it is operating on a temp home directory and never touches `~`.
  - Include an intentional failure variant to verify artifact upload (diffs/before-after) works.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","title":"Docs smoke tests: quickstart commands stay executable in CI","description":"# Task: Docs smoke tests (quickstart stays executable)

## Goal
Prevent docs drift by ensuring the README/quickstart instructions remain executable and safe.

This is a user-trust feature: if the quickstart breaks, new users churn.

## Scope
- Focus on commands that docs will recommend for a fresh install:
  - `wa --help`
  - `wa --version --verbose`
  - `wa robot quick-start`
  - `wa setup --dry-run` (in a temp HOME)
  - `wa doctor` (in a minimal environment; may be expected to warn but must be actionable)

- For commands that require a real WezTerm environment, docs should point to the E2E scripts as the canonical tested path rather than embedding fragile “just run this” snippets.

## Deliverables
1) A small docs-smoke runner (CI-friendly)
- Runs in a temp HOME + temp workspace.
- Captures stdout/stderr to artifacts.
- Never modifies real user dotfiles.

2) A mapping from “docs claims” → “test coverage”
- For each quickstart step, link to either:
  - a docs-smoke command, or
  - an E2E script case (e.g., `wa-4vx.10.7`, `wa-nu4.3.3.10`).

3) CI integration
- Add a CI job/step that runs docs smoke and uploads artifacts on failure.

## Logging & artifacts (must match the E2E philosophy)
- Artifacts directory contains:
  - `env.txt` (OS, rustc, cargo, enabled features)
  - `commands.txt` (exact commands executed)
  - `stdout.txt` / `stderr.txt` per command (or per step)
  - `summary.json` (pass/fail + durations)

- Logs must never contain secrets (even if users have real configs on their machine).

## Testing
- Unit-ish tests:
  - if docs smoke is implemented as a Rust subcommand, validate output schemas and deterministic ordering.
  - if implemented as a shell script, include at least one self-test that verifies it creates artifacts and returns correct exit codes.

- CI tests:
  - docs smoke runs on every PR and fails when a quickstart command regresses.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs smoke runs in CI and fails when a referenced quickstart command breaks.
- Failures are diagnosable from artifacts without rerunning locally.


## Testing
- “Tests for the tests”:
  - include one intentionally-failing step under a special flag (or a dedicated CI mode) to ensure artifact capture + summaries are working.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"GrayRidge","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:42:24.357386135Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:27:37.935389737Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-ci","area-docs","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.4","title":"[EPIC] Phase 5: Advanced — vendoring, streaming, distributed mode, coordination","description":"# Phase 5 — Advanced Features

## Objective
Push wa from “solid local orchestrator” into “fleet-grade system”:
- Selective WezTerm vendoring (feature-gated) for streaming output / lower overhead.
- Real-time output streaming (reduce polling and gaps).
- Optional distributed mode: `wa-agent` near mux servers + central aggregator.
- Multi-agent coordination helpers.

## Why this phase exists
The core system can ship without this. These features are justified only when they deliver measurable wins:
- latency, correctness (fewer gaps), or usability.

## Deliverables
- Vendored client prototype + benchmarks vs CLI.
- If ROI confirmed: ship `--features vendored` with strict version checks.
- Distributed protocol design + first implementation.

## Acceptance criteria
- Benchmarks show clear advantage for vendored/streaming modes.
- Distributed mode can ingest deltas from at least one remote domain without stalling the central watcher.



## Success Criteria
- Advanced capabilities (vendoring, streaming, distributed mode, coordination) remain optional and do not compromise core safety.
- Each advanced feature has clear boundaries, tests, and safe defaults.


## Testing
- Performance ROI validation:
  - Benchmarks must prove vendored/streaming modes are meaningfully better than CLI polling for target workloads.

- Safety/regression:
  - Optional features must compile-gate cleanly and have their own unit/integration tests.

- E2E (when distributed mode is implemented):
  - Distributed E2E tests must validate persistence, ordering, and secret-safe artifacts (`wa-nu4.4.3.5`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.4.1","title":"[EPIC] Selective WezTerm vendoring (feature vendored) + version compatibility","description":"# [EPIC] Selective WezTerm vendoring

## Mission
Unlock capabilities not available via `wezterm cli`, while preserving a CLI-first default:
- zero-copy scrollback access
- real-time output streaming (enables true subscription vs polling)

## Default posture
- CLI-first remains the foundation.
- Vendoring is an **optional acceleration lane** behind `--features vendored`.

## Key risks
- Upstream API churn
- Version compatibility problems
- Larger binary / more complex build

## Definition of done
- `--features vendored` builds successfully.
- wa can detect local WezTerm version compatibility and surface it in `wa doctor`.
- Unified client picks best available backend (vendored when compatible, otherwise CLI).


## Success Criteria
- Vendored WezTerm components are minimal, clearly version-pinned, and do not break upstream compatibility expectations.
- CI validates vendor build and ensures core features still work without vendoring enabled.


## Testing
- Feature-gate tests:
  - Ensure `--features vendored` builds and unit tests run, and `--no-default-features` (or default) still works without pulling vendored deps.

- Compatibility tests:
  - Fixture-based tests for version/commit matching logic (supported/unsupported versions).
  - `wa doctor` should surface compatibility deterministically.

- RPC smoke tests:
  - Basic mux RPC framing tests with fixtures so failures are diagnosable without a live WezTerm.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:36:22.401746449Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T15:44:05.288907533Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-vendored","area-wezterm","phase-5"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.4.1.1","title":"Vendored feature scaffolding: add wezterm crates + compile behind feature flag","description":"# Task: Vendored feature scaffolding

## Goal
Add the minimal dependency + feature wiring to compile vendored WezTerm crates behind `--features vendored`.

## Deliverables
- Cargo feature `vendored`.
- Optional dependencies on the selected WezTerm crates (selective, not full fork).
- A build that succeeds in both modes:
  - default (CLI-only)
  - vendored

## Testing
- Build/test matrix:
  - `cargo check --all-targets` (default)
  - `cargo check --all-targets --features vendored`
  - ideally `cargo test --features vendored` once minimal unit tests exist

- Ensure feature gating is strict:
  - enabling `vendored` must not pull heavy deps into default builds

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo build` works without vendored deps.
- `cargo build --features vendored` works and does not affect default behavior.
